title: System, method, and computer program for verifying virtual network function (vnf) package and/or network service definition integrity
abstract: A system, method, and computer program product are provided for verifying virtual network function (VNF) package and/or network service definition integrity. In use, a system identifies a virtual network function package or a network service definition for performing integrity verification. The system computes a unique identifier of the VNF package or the network service definition that allows verification of an integrity of the VNF package or the network service definition. The system stores the unique identifier in a blockchain or a shared database. The system provides the VNF package or the network service definition to an entity such that the entity is capable of verifying the integrity of the VNF package or the network service definition by using the unique identifier of the VNF package or the network service definition from the blockchain or the shared database.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method, comprising: identifying, by a system, a virtual network function (VNF) package or a network service definition for performing integrity verification; computing, by the system, a unique identifier of the VNF package or the network service definition that allows verification of an integrity of the VNF package or the network service definition; storing, by the system, the unique identifier in a blockchain or a shared database; and providing, by the system, the VNF package or the network service definition to an entity such that the entity is capable of verifying the integrity of the VNF package or the network service definition by using the unique identifier of the VNF package or the network service definition from the blockchain or the shared database. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the system includes a plurality of entities each dedicated to performing a portion of the integrity verification. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein the system includes one entity dedicated to performing the integrity verification. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein a verified VNF package, resulting from the entity verifying the integrity of the VNF package, is added to a communication service provider (CSP) catalog. 5. The method of claim 4, wherein the system verifies that the verified VNF package in the CSP catalog is unchanged by verifying the unique identifier of the VNF package in the blockchain or the shared database. 6. The method of claim 1, wherein a verified network service definition, resulting from the entity verifying the integrity of the network service definition, is added to a CSP catalog. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein an orchestrator verifies that the verified network service definition in the CSP catalog is unchanged by verifying the unique identifier of the network service definition in the blockchain or the shared database. 8. The method of claim 6, wherein an orchestrator verifies that the verified network service definition in the CSP catalog is unchanged by verifying the unique identifier of the network service definition in the blockchain or the shared database when the orchestrator receives a request to run the network service definition. 9. The method of claim 8, wherein the orchestrator creates a running network service utilizing the verified network service definition. 10. The method of claim 9, further comprising: computing, by the orchestrator, a first unique identifier for a configuration representation associated with the running network service; storing, by the orchestrator, the first unique identifier in the blockchain or the shared database; computing, by the orchestrator, a second unique identifier for the running network service during production; comparing, by the orchestrator, the second unique identifier to the first unique identifier stored in the blockchain or the shared database; determining, by the orchestrator, whether the second unique identifier matches the first unique identifier; and generating, by the orchestrator, an alert for potential tampering of the running network service if the second unique identifier does not match the first unique identifier. 11. The method of claim 10, further comprising updating and storing, by the orchestrator, the first unique identifier if the running network service undergoes an approved change. 12. The method of claim 11, wherein the approved change includes one or more of migration, change of scaling, change of network address, change of location, change of functional configuration, and change of one or more policies. 13. The method of claim 1, wherein the entity is a communication service provider. 14. The method of claim 1, wherein the entity is a system. 15. The method of claim 1, wherein the entity is a system associated with a communication service provider. 16. A computer program product embodied on a non- transitory computer readable medium, comprising computer code for: identifying, by a system, a virtual network function (VNF) package or a network service definition for performing integrity verification; computing, by the system, a unique identifier of the VNF package or the network service definition that allows verification of an integrity of the VNF package or the network service definition; storing, by the system, the unique identifier in a blockchain or a shared database; and providing, by the system, the VNF package or the network service definition to an entity such that the entity is capable of verifying the integrity of the VNF package or the network service definition by using the unique identifier of the VNF package or the network service definition from the blockchain or the shared database. 17. The computer program product of claim 16, wherein the system includes a plurality of entities each dedicated to performing a portion of the integrity verification. 18. The computer program product of claim 16, wherein the system includes one entity dedicated to performing the integrity verification. 19. The computer program product of claim 16, wherein a verified VNF package, resulting from the entity verifying the integrity of the VNF package, is added to a communication service provider (CSP) catalog. 20. A system, comprising one or more processors, operable for: identifying, by a system, a virtual network function (VNF) package or a network service definition for performing integrity verification; computing, by the system, a unique identifier of the VNF package or the network service definition that allows verification of an integrity of the VNF package or the network service definition; storing, by the system, the unique identifier in a blockchain or a shared database; and providing, by the system, the VNF package or the network service definition to an entity such that the entity is capable of verifying the integrity of the VNF package or the network service definition by using the unique identifier of the VNF package or the network service definition from the blockchain or the shared database.
